Phase-44.NAME: Email Onboarding UX and Password Recovery
Phase-44.purpose: Improve registration trust and supportability by redesigning verification emails, clarifying check-email UX, enforcing strong passwords, adding password reset, and handling duplicate email registration clearly.

scope.in: Beautiful verification email template, password reset email template, operator note for sender avatar and deliverability, spam-folder hint in the pending registration block, removal of stale trial/VPN wording, removal of the registration right-side explanatory panel, strong-password policy, forgot/reset password flow, and duplicate-email recovery CTA.
scope.out: Deploy/install script changes, paid billing changes, first-handshake trial activation, cookie-only auth migration, and any guarantee that all mail clients will render a sender avatar.

operator-notes.note-1: Sender avatar is not a reliable backend email payload feature. KrotPN should document provider/mailbox-side options such as sender mailbox profile, domain branding, BIMI/DMARC readiness where applicable, and Resend domain verification, but code must not claim it can force avatars in every recipient mailbox.
operator-notes.note-2: Spam placement cannot be eliminated by frontend code. The product fix is twofold: improve domain/provider deliverability and make the UI tell users to check spam if the email is missing.

steps.step-1: Redesign verification email HTML/text templates with KrotPN branding, compact Russian copy, safe button/link fallback, and no token exposure in logs.
steps.step-2: Add password reset email template and document the sender-avatar/deliverability operator boundary for Resend-backed mail.
steps.step-3: Update registration pending UI: add spam-folder guidance, remove "trial на 3 дня и VPN-доступ", and remove the right-side explanatory block.
steps.step-4: Implement shared strong-password policy with backend enforcement and frontend strength feedback for registration and password reset.
steps.step-5: Add one-time password reset token storage, request endpoint, reset endpoint, TTL, replay protection, and rate-limit hooks.
steps.step-6: Add forgot-password and reset-password frontend screens with compact mobile-first layout and safe success/error states.
steps.step-7: Return explicit duplicate-email registration guidance with a reset-password CTA while keeping throttling and safe logs.
steps.step-8: Run backend auth/email tests, frontend build, Phase-44 smoke, XML parse, MyGRACE lint, and verify no deploy/install files changed.

data-flow.flow-1: User registers -> M-041 creates pending registration -> M-040 sends branded verification email -> M-009 pending screen tells user to check inbox and spam.
data-flow.flow-2: User forgets password -> M-002 validates request shape and throttling -> M-040 sends reset email -> M-002 consumes token once -> M-027 password hash update.
data-flow.flow-3: Duplicate registration -> M-041 identifies existing email -> M-009 shows account-exists message and reset CTA without creating pending side effects.

mental-walkthroughs.walkthrough-1: Normal registration: user enters strong password, sees check-email state with spam hint, verifies email, and proceeds without stale 3-day wording.
mental-walkthroughs.walkthrough-2: Weak password: frontend warns early, backend rejects deterministically, no pending registration or provider email is created.
mental-walkthroughs.walkthrough-3: Forgot password: user requests reset, receives email, token works once, replay fails, and no token or raw email provider payload appears in logs.

phase-gates.gate-1: Verification and reset email templates render HTML and text fallback and never log tokens or provider secrets.
phase-gates.gate-2: Registration pending UI includes spam-folder guidance and contains no "trial на 3 дня", immediate VPN access promise, or removed right-side info panel.
phase-gates.gate-3: Strong-password policy is enforced server-side and reflected client-side.
phase-gates.gate-4: Password reset tokens are one-time, TTL-bound, and replay-safe.
phase-gates.gate-5: Duplicate-email registration clearly offers password recovery.

risks.risk-1: Password reset expands the account takeover surface if token TTL, replay checks, or throttling are weak.
risks.risk-2: Explicit duplicate-email messaging improves UX but increases enumeration clarity; throttling and safe provider responses are required.
risks.risk-3: Email deliverability and avatars are partly external to KrotPN and must be documented honestly.

commands.command: cd backend && SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key-with-enough-length DATA_ENCRYPTION_KEY=dpFmfSVqfdAK3yx5MQV6Tcv35RZuw5MzaH0yhxJ01q0= PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_email_delivery.py tests/test_verified_registration.py tests/test_password_recovery.py tests/test_email_verification_guard.py tests/test_db_migrations.py -v

python3 -m xml.etree.ElementTree docs/plans/Phase-44.xml docs/modules/M-062.xml docs/modules/M-002.xml docs/modules/M-009.xml docs/modules/M-028.xml docs/modules/M-040.xml docs/verification/V-M-062.xml docs/verification/V-M-002.xml docs/verification/V-M-009.xml docs/verification/V-M-028.xml docs/verification/V-M-040.xml docs/graph-index.xml docs/plan-index.xml docs/verification-index.xml docs/requirements.xml docs/current-status.xml

evidence.evidence-1: 2026-06-01: backend Phase-44 auth/email/migration tests passed outside sandbox because sandbox aiosqlite in-memory DB hangs; result was 29 passed in 0.84s.
evidence.evidence-2: 2026-06-01: frontend npm run build passed and node scripts/phase44-auth-email-ux-smoke.mjs passed.
evidence.evidence-3: 2026-06-01: XML parse and MyGRACE lint passed.
evidence.evidence-4: deploy/install scripts were intentionally untouched by Phase-44 execution.
